title: anchusa officinalis subsp. officinalis
intro: Anchusa officinalis subsp. officinalis, also known as Alpine Bugloss, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the European Alps. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, ease respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Anchusa officinalis subsp. officinalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
